variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pop/pop.py — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each next line is a non-negative integer that fits in a 64-bit unsigned integer (up to 2^64 - 1). For each, output the number of set bits on its own line. Implement popcount yourself — do not use any language-builtin population-count intrinsic or a string-conversion bit-count shortcut. Pure Python; no numpy.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pop; python3 -c 'import re,sys; f,lang=sys.argv[1],sys.argv[2]; pats=sys.argv[3:]; src=open(f).read(); src=re.sub(r"/\*.*?\*/"," ",src,flags=re.S); L=[re.sub(r"\"(?:\\.|[^\"\\])*\""," ",(re.sub(r"#.*$","",ln) if lang.startswith("py") else re.sub(r"//.*$","",ln))) for ln in src.split(chr(10))]; s=chr(10).join(L); sys.exit(1 if any(re.search(p,s) for p in pats) else 0)' pop.py python '\.bit_count\s*\(' 'bin\s*\(.*\)\s*\.count\s*\(' 'format\s*\(.*\)\s*\.count\s*\(' || { echo "forbidden api"; exit 1; }; python3 pop.py < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pop/pop.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each next line is a non-negative integer that fits in a 64-bit unsigned integer (up to 2^64 - 1). validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pop; python3 -c 'import re,sys; f,lang=sys.argv[1],sys.argv[2]; pats=sys.argv[3:]; src=open(f).read(); src=re.sub(r"/\*.*?\*/"," ",src,flags=re.S); L=[re.sub(r"\"(?:\\.|[^\"\\])*\""," ",(re.sub(r"#.*$","",ln) if lang.startswith("py") else re.sub(r"//.*$","",ln))) for ln in src.split(chr(10))]; s=chr(10).join(L); sys.exit(1 if any(re.search(p,s) for p in pats) else 0)' pop.go go 'bits\.OnesCount' || { echo "forbidden api"; exit 1; }; go build -o pop pop.go && ./pop < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pop/pop.rs — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each next line is a non-negative integer that fits in a 64-bit unsigned integer (up to 2^64 - 1). For each, output the number of set bits on its own line. Implement popcount yourself — do not use any language-builtin population-count intrinsic or a string-conversion bit-count shortcut. Idiomatic Rust 2021+; compile with `rustc -O pop.rs -o pop`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pop; python3 -c 'import re,sys; f,lang=sys.argv[1],sys.argv[2]; pats=sys.argv[3:]; src=open(f).read(); src=re.sub(r"/\*.*?\*/"," ",src,flags=re.S); L=[re.sub(r"\"(?:\\.|[^\"\\])*\""," ",(re.sub(r"#.*$","",ln) if lang.startswith("py") else re.sub(r"//.*$","",ln))) for ln in src.split(chr(10))]; s=chr(10).join(L); sys.exit(1 if any(re.search(p,s) for p in pats) else 0)' pop.rs rust '\.count_ones\s*\(' || { echo "forbidden api"; exit 1; }; rustc -O pop.rs -o pop 2>/dev/null && ./pop < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pop/pop.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each next line is a non-negative integer that fits in a 64-bit unsigned integer (up to 2^64 - 1). For each, output the number of set bits on its own line. Implement popcount yourself — do not use any language-builtin population-count intrinsic or a string-conversion bit-count shortcut. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast pop.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pop; python3 -c 'import re,sys; f,lang=sys.argv[1],sys.argv[2]; pats=sys.argv[3:]; src=open(f).read(); src=re.sub(r"/\*.*?\*/"," ",src,flags=re.S); L=[re.sub(r"\"(?:\\.|[^\"\\])*\""," ",(re.sub(r"#.*$","",ln) if lang.startswith("py") else re.sub(r"//.*$","",ln))) for ln in src.split(chr(10))]; s=chr(10).join(L); sys.exit(1 if any(re.search(p,s) for p in pats) else 0)' pop.zig zig '@popCount' || { echo "forbidden api"; exit 1; }; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast pop.zig && ./pop < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
